prompt: Can you review this email I'm about to send my skip-level? 'Hi Sarah, I hope you're doing well. I wanted to follow up on our last discussion around team capacity. As you know, Q4 has been really intense and the team has been stretched. We've had a few conversations internally about priorities. Given all of this context, I think it might be worth exploring whether we could potentially consider adding a headcount in the data engineering area in the next planning cycle if the budget allows.' What do you think?
assertions: The rewritten email has a specific, informative subject line (not generic like 'Follow-up' or 'Team capacity')
The headcount request appears in the first sentence or two of the rewritten body
Preamble phrases ('I hope you're doing well', 'As you know', 'Q4 has been intense') are removed or substantially shortened
The passive hedging ('might be worth exploring whether we could potentially consider') is made direct
The feedback explains *why* the original is problematic from the reader's perspective, not just labels it
